concerns.text: Task T020 (compute correlation matrices) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depends on the output of T013 (time-series extraction). T013 is not marked [P] and implies a sequential data-flow dependency. T020 cannot run in parallel with T013 or other T013 instances without the time-series artifact. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule and the definition of [P] tags.

Task T030 (prepare feature matrix) is marked [P] but depends on the output of T021 (centrality metrics) and T024 (group comparison results). The feature matrix requires the computed centrality values which are produced in T021. Marking T030 as [P] implies it has no dependencies on other tasks, which is false. It must wait for US2 completion.

The 'Independent Test' section for US1 lists T010 and T011 as tests to be written. However, T012 (download) and T013 (preprocess) are the implementation tasks. The ordering implies tests are written before implementation (TDD), but the task list places T010/T011 *after* the 'Tests for User Story 1' header but *before* the 'Implementation' header. While TDD is good, the [P] tag on T010/T011 suggests they can run in parallel with T012/T013, which is impossible as tests consume the artifacts produced by T012/T013. The [P] tag on test tasks is misleading if they depend on the implementation tasks in the same story.

Task T025 (sensitivity analysis) depends on T024 (group comparison results) and T020/T021 (centrality computation). T025 is not marked [P], which is correct, but the dependency chain is dense. The text 'sweep thresholds' implies a loop over T020/T021 logic. Ensure T025 is explicitly ordered after T024 in the execution plan, as it consumes the 'significant nodes' logic from T024.
